svg.title: OralCare-AI Complete Architecture Story
svg.desc: Comprehensive OralCare-AI architecture diagram showing users, frontend, backend, AI modalities, fusion, XAI, RAG chatbot, database, reports, tech stack, terminology, and why each layer is used.
svg.text: Multimodal oral cancer decision-support: evidence ingestion, AI models, fusion, explainability, doctor approval, patient communication, and RAG chatbot.
Oral cancer evidence is fragmented across images, pathology, genomics, clinical history, appointments, and reports. A one-time classifier does not match the real clinical journey.
A doctor-in-the-loop, explainable multimodal workflow that converts evidence into risk trends, validated reports, and simple patient answers.
Patient, doctor, technician, chat, reports, appointments
Routes data to AI, fusion, DB, RAG, and reports
Each modality becomes a standard ModuleOutput
Why: lightweight, fast, good transfer-learning baseline
Why: higher capacity for tissue morphology
ModuleOutput = prediction + confidence + embedding + warnings + explanations + quality flags
This decouples model internals from the fusion layer and makes the system modular.
Evidence-weighted late fusion, missing-modality awareness, doctor-validatable explanations
Combines decisions after each modality model
Why: safer without true patient-matched multimodal data
Works with any subset of 4 modalities
Marks missing evidence and lowers confidence
Shows which modality influenced the result
Output: fused risk score, risk class, confidence, warnings, trace, what-if, modality proportions
Clinical memory, vector context, and grounded chatbot retrieval
Earlier review, explainable evidence, patient clarity, continuous monitoring
Use as hackathon/demo metrics; replace with locked test-set metrics before clinical publication.
Closing pitch: OralCare-AI is not just a cancer classifier; it is the explainable workflow around it, turning fragmented evidence into doctor-reviewed action faster.
Detailed Flow: Parallel Modality Processing to Fusion
All four models run independently in parallel. Fusion starts only after available modality outputs are normalized into standard evidence packets.
Parallel design: missing models do not block the pipeline; available ModuleOutputs are fused, missing modalities are flagged, and confidence reflects evidence coverage.
Late fusion = combine decisions after individual models
RAG = Retrieval-Augmented Generation for grounded answers
Doctor-in-the-loop = clinician validates before patient result
